abstract: Specify the attributes of a transform.
overview:
## Overview

Use these keys and values when accessing transform attributes by name, such as with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTransformSetAttribute(_:_:_:_:)] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTransformGetAttribute(_:_:)] functions. You can also use some of the values directly in certain function calls, such as when you create a encode transform with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecEncodeTransformCreate(_:_:)] function and give it an `encodeType` parameter to seed the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecEncodeTypeAttribute] attribute.

# Transform Attributes

**API Collection**

Specify the attributes of a transform.

## Overview

Use these keys and values when accessing transform attributes by name, such as with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTransformSetAttribute(_:_:_:_:)] and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTransformGetAttribute(_:_:)] functions. You can also use some of the values directly in certain function calls, such as when you create a encode transform with the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecEncodeTransformCreate(_:_:)] function and give it an `encodeType` parameter to seed the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecEncodeTypeAttribute] attribute.

## Encode and Decode Keys

- **kSecEncodeLineLengthAttribute**: The length of encoded Base32 or Base64 lines.
- **kSecEncodeTypeAttribute**: The encoding used by an encode transform.
- **kSecDecodeTypeAttribute**: The encoding used by a decode transform.
- **kSecCompressionRatio**: The compression ratio.

## Digest and Encryption Keys

- **kSecDigestTypeAttribute**: The digest algorithm.
- **kSecDigestLengthAttribute**: The digest length.
- **kSecDigestHMACKeyAttribute**: The key for HMAC operation.
- **kSecInputIsAttributeName**: The type of input to the transform.
- **kSecEncryptionMode**: The encryption mode.
- **kSecEncryptKey**: The encryption key for the transform.
- **kSecIVKey**: The setting for an initialization vector.
- **kSecPaddingKey**: The kind of padding to use.
- **kSecOAEPEncodingParametersAttributeName**: The OAEP encoding parameters.
- **kSecOAEPMGF1DigestAlgorithmAttributeName**: The OAEP MGF1 digest algorithm.
- **kSecOAEPMessageLengthAttributeName**: The OAEP message length.

## Transform Keys

- **kSecTransformInputAttributeName**: The input to a transform.
- **kSecTransformOutputAttributeName**: The output of a transform.
- **kSecTransformDebugAttributeName**: A write stream that should receive debug data.
- **kSecKeyAttributeName**: The cryptographic key associated with a transform.
- **kSecSignatureAttributeName**: The cryptographic signature associated with a transform.
- **kSecTransformAbortAttributeName**: The reason for an abort.
- **kSecTransformTransformName**: The name of a transform.

## Encode Types

- **kSecBase32Encoding**: A base 32 encoding.
- **kSecBase64Encoding**: A base 64 encoding.
- **kSecZLibEncoding**: A compressed encoding.

## Digest Types

- **kSecDigestMD2**: An MD2 digest.
- **kSecDigestMD4**: An MD4 digest.
- **kSecDigestMD5**: An MD5 digest.
- **kSecDigestSHA1**: An SHA1 digest.
- **kSecDigestSHA2**: An SHA2 digest.
- **kSecDigestHMACMD5**: An HMAC using the MD5 digest algorithm.
- **kSecDigestHMACSHA1**: An HMAC using the SHA1 digest algorithm.
- **kSecDigestHMACSHA2**: An HMAC using one of the SHA2 digest algorithms.

## Line Lengths

- **kSecLineLength64**: A line length of 64 bytes.
- **kSecLineLength76**: A line length of 76 bytes.

## Input Types

- **kSecInputIsDigest**: The input is a digest of the original data.
- **kSecInputIsPlainText**: The input is plain text.
- **kSecInputIsRaw**: The input is raw.

## Padding Types

- **kSecPaddingNoneKey**: No padding will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecPaddingOAEPKey**: PKCS7 padding will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecPaddingPKCS1Key**: PKCS1 padding will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecPaddingPKCS5Key**: PKCS5 padding will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecPaddingPKCS7Key**: PKCS7 padding will be used when encrypting or decrypting.

## Encryption Modes

- **kSecModeNoneKey**: No mode will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecModeCBCKey**: CBC mode will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecModeCFBKey**: CFB mode will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecModeECBKey**: ECB mode will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
- **kSecModeOFBKey**: OFB mode will be used when encrypting or decrypting.
